extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 14km opening ITT by 24 seconds over EVENEPOEL, with POGACAR third at 33 seconds despite his 49km solo effort at Strade Bianche two days prior.
tactical_patterns: GANNA aggressively hit apexes at the halfway turnaround point, nearly clipping barriers, suggesting risk-taking pays dividends on technical turns.
POGACAR's Strade Bianche 49km solo two days prior had no visible impact on ITT form, dominating GC rivals despite expected fatigue.
EVENEPOEL lost 9 seconds between halfway split (2 seconds down) and finish, indicating second-half pacing issues on sub-15km efforts.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 24-second margin over EVENEPOEL on 14km course suggests peak early-season TT form despite technical zigzagging around manholes
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Second place but faded 9 seconds in second half after being only 2 down at split
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Third place and dominant among GC contenders despite minimal recovery from 49km Strade Bianche solo, signaling exceptional recovery capacity
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: negative: Fourth at 40+ seconds despite being described as spectacular time trialist, suggests flat power courses under 14km not his optimal distance
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: neutral: 17 seconds behind benchmark time hints at internal INEOS leadership ambiguity still unresolved
